title: Fagraea wallichiana
taxonomy: Fagraea wallichiana is classified within the family Loganiaceae. This family is a part of the larger order Gentianales. The Loganiaceae family comprises about 100 species of flowering plants, mainly found in tropical regions. Fagraea wallichiana is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Fagraea.
uses: Fagraea wallichiana is a plant species known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The leaves and bark of Fagraea wallichiana have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. Fagraea wallichiana is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Fagraea wallichiana for medicinal use.
